pattern: Because each move adds the current larger coordinate, values grow at least geometrically, so a bounded breadth-first search that prunes anything past the target settles in only a logarithmic number of layers.
hints: When the larger coordinate is positive every move roughly doubles a coordinate, so the target is reached in very few moves if at all.
Search forward from the start, but discard any state whose x exceeds tx or y exceeds ty since coordinates never shrink.
Run BFS adding states (x+m, y) and (x, y+m) with m = max(x,y), dedup visited cells, and return the first depth that lands exactly on (tx, ty); a stuck (0,0) start reaches only itself.

stress.description: construct a reachable target by replaying random moves, occasionally an unreachable one
stress.note: replayed moves guarantee many reachable cases; perturbation tests -1 paths
